name: messages
context: Send prompts to Claude via the Messages API — multi-turn conversation, vision, tool use, and structured output
info: The Messages API (`POST /v1/messages`) is the primary interface for interacting with Claude. You send an array of messages with alternating user/assistant roles and receive a response with content blocks (text, tool_use, thinking). The API supports system prompts, temperature/top_p/top_k sampling, stop sequences, tool definitions, structured output via JSON Schema, vision (images and PDFs), and extended thinking with configurable budget tokens. All requests require `model`, `max_tokens`, and `messages` parameters. Responses include `id`, `content`, `stop_reason`, and `usage` with token counts. Sub-skills [[anthropic/messages/create]] and [[anthropic/messages/stream]] cover synchronous and streaming variants.
triggers: send a prompt to the anthropic api
body:
# Messages API

The Messages API is Claude's primary interface. Every interaction — from a simple question to a complex multi-turn agentic workflow — goes through `POST /v1/messages`.

## Endpoint

```
POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages
```

## Required Headers

```
x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY
anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
content-type: application/json
```

## Required Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `model` | string | Model ID (e.g., `claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5`) |
| `max_tokens` | integer | Maximum tokens to generate |
| `messages` | array | Alternating user/assistant message objects |

## Optional Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `system` | string/array | — | System prompt (text or content block array) |
| `temperature` | float | 1.0 | Randomness (0.0 = deterministic, 1.0 = creative) |
| `top_p` | float | — | Nucleus sampling (use OR temperature) |
| `top_k` | integer | — | Sample from top K options |
| `stop_sequences` | array | — | Custom stop strings |
| `stream` | boolean | false | Enable SSE streaming |
| `tools` | array | — | Tool definitions for [[anthropic/tool-use]] |
| `tool_choice` | object | — | Control tool usage: auto, any, tool, none |
| `metadata` | object | — | Request metadata (user_id for abuse detection) |
| `thinking` | object | — | Extended thinking config with budget_tokens |

## Message Structure

```json
{
  "role": "user",
  "content": [
    { "type": "text", "text": "Describe this image" },
    { "type": "image", "source": { "type": "base64", "media_type": "image/png", "data": "..." } }
  ]
}
```

Content can be a plain string or an array of typed blocks: `text`, `image`, `document`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`.

## Response Structure

```json
{
  "id": "msg_01XFDUDYJgAACzvnptvVoYEL",
  "type": "message",
  "role": "assistant",
  "content": [
    { "type": "text", "text": "Hello! How can I help you?" }
  ],
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "stop_reason": "end_turn",
  "usage": {
    "input_tokens": 12,
    "output_tokens": 8
  }
}
```

## Stop Reasons

| Value | Meaning |
|-------|----------|
| `end_turn` | Claude finished naturally |
| `stop_sequence` | Hit a custom stop sequence |
| `max_tokens` | Reached max_tokens limit |
| `tool_use` | Claude wants to call a tool |

## Python Example

```python
import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    system="You are a helpful coding assistant.",
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Write a Python function to check if a number is prime"}
    ]
)
print(message.content[0].text)
```

## TypeScript Example

```typescript
import Anthropic from "@anthropic-ai/sdk";

const client = new Anthropic();
const message = await client.messages.create({
  model: "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  max_tokens: 1024,
  system: "You are a helpful coding assistant.",
  messages: [
    { role: "user", content: "Write a TypeScript function to check if a number is prime" }
  ]
});
console.log(message.content[0].text);
```

## Multi-Turn Conversation

```python
messages = [
    {"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of France?"},
    {"role": "assistant", "content": "The capital of France is Paris."},
    {"role": "user", "content": "What is its population?"}
]

response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=messages
)
```

The API is stateless — you send the full conversation history each time. Use [[anthropic/prompt-caching]] to avoid re-processing repeated prefixes.

## Vision (Images & Documents)

```python
import base64

with open("image.png", "rb") as f:
    image_data = base64.standard_b64encode(f.read()).decode("utf-8")

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{
        "role": "user",
        "content": [
            {"type": "image", "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": "image/png", "data": image_data}},
            {"type": "text", "text": "What is in this image?"}
        ]
    }]
)
```

Supported image formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP. PDF documents via `type: "document"`.

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | API key (SDKs read this automatically) |

sections.title: Request format
sections.body: Send `POST /v1/messages` with JSON body containing `model`, `max_tokens`, and `messages`. Messages alternate between `user` and `assistant` roles. Content is either a plain string or an array of typed content blocks (`text`, `image`, `document`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`). The API supports up to 100,000 messages per request. See [[anthropic/auth]] for required headers.

sections.title: Response format
sections.body: Responses include `id` (unique message ID prefixed `msg_`), `role` (always `assistant`), `content` (array of content blocks), `model`, `stop_reason` (end_turn, stop_sequence, max_tokens, tool_use), and `usage` with `input_tokens` and `output_tokens`. When [[anthropic/prompt-caching]] is active, usage also includes `cache_creation_input_tokens` and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

sections.title: Vision and document processing
sections.body: Send images as base64-encoded content blocks with `type: "image"` or as URLs. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP. Send PDFs with `type: "document"` and `source.media_type: "application/pdf"`. All current Claude models support vision. Images are resized automatically to fit within token limits.

sections.title: Extended thinking
sections.body: Enable extended thinking by setting `thinking: { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: 10000 }`. Claude will include `thinking` content blocks in the response showing its reasoning process. Budget tokens set the maximum thinking length. Extended thinking improves performance on complex reasoning, math, and coding tasks. Supported on Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, and Haiku 4.5.
